idea: Unemployment Claims Volatility Spike Reversal Trade

data.source: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims weekly) via fred_series adapter

behavior: When weekly initial jobless claims spike unexpectedly above their 4-week moving average by more than 15%, labor market shock creates mean-reversion pressure. Claims typically normalize within 2-4 weeks unless structural deterioration is occurring.

market_impact.rationale: Claims spikes signal potential Fed rate-cut expectations and flight-to-safety rotation into financials as bond yields compress.

trade_logic.entry: Initial claims breach 4-week MA by 15%+ on weekly close; enter long XLF
trade_logic.exit: Claims return within 5% of 4-week MA OR after 3 weeks, whichever comes first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED jobless claims data updates weekly; volatility spikes occur monthly on seasonal and unexpected labor market moves; reversion typically completes within 21 days.

idea: Natural Gas Futures Volatility Pump Rotation

data.source: EIA Natural Gas Storage Weekly (via html_table adapter) paired with price_only daily futures proxy

behavior: Unusual weekly EIA storage draws (>10% above seasonal norm) combined with rising futures volatility trigger energy sector rotation; energy stocks often lag futures by 2-5 trading days due to portfolio rebalancing.

market_impact.rationale: Input cost shocks typically compress energy sector valuations but benefit large integrated producers on margin expansion when commodities spike.

trade_logic.entry: EIA weekly storage draw >10% above 5-year seasonal median; natural gas futures up 2%+ on day of release; enter long XLE at close
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR once natural gas futures reverse 3% from entry, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EIA releases storage weekly; abnormal draws occur 3-4 times per quarter; futures volatility spikes regularly; price action lags trigger by defined window.

idea: Regional Earthquake Cluster Insurance Demand Spike

data.source: USGS earthquake_activity daily feed (magnitude 3.5+) via earthquake_activity adapter; paired with Google Trends searches for 'earthquake insurance' and 'home inspection'

behavior: Clusters of 4+ earthquakes in 7 days (any US region, magnitude 3.5+) trigger 40-60% surge in insurance/inspection searches within 48 hours. Real estate and property stocks (builders, insurers) experience brief bullish pressure as emergency repair demand rises.

market_impact.rationale: Earthquake clusters drive near-term demand for property inspections, repairs, and insurance policy uptake; benefits regional real estate and construction activity.

trade_logic.entry: 4+ magnitude 3.5+ earthquakes within 7 days in same US region; Google Trends 'earthquake insurance' index rises 40%+ week-over-week; enter long XLRE
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days OR Google Trends 'earthquake insurance' returns to baseline, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: USGS earthquake data is continuous; regional clusters occur 2-3 times per quarter; Google Trends lags by 1-2 days; 5-day position window ensures frequent fire opportunities.

data.source: OpenChargeMap total charger count (daily via openchargemap adapter) paired with price_only XLI daily momentum

behavior: When weekly net new charger additions exceed prior 8-week rolling average by 20%+, it signals infrastructure investment acceleration. Industrial and energy stocks respond bullishly within 3-7 days as construction/manufacturing demand rises.

market_impact.rationale: Charger buildout expansion signals sustained capex cycle in industrial manufacturing, electrical equipment, and construction services.

trade_logic.entry: Weekly net charger adds (vs 8-week rolling avg) spike 20%+; XLI volume above 90th percentile; enter long XLI at next day's open
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days OR if weekly charger growth rate drops below 5%, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: OpenChargeMap updates daily; charger deployment accelerations occur monthly; industrial sector responds predictably to capex cycle signals.

data.source: FRED series TRFVOLUSM157N (Truck freight volume monthly) via fred_series adapter

behavior: When monthly truck freight volume rebounds 3%+ from its lowest point in a 6-month window, it signals logistics demand recovery. Industrial and consumer discretionary sectors experience 2-4 week rallies as inventory replenishment accelerates.

market_impact.rationale: Freight recovery is a leading indicator of production and consumption rebound; benefits industrial equipment and logistics operators.

trade_logic.entry: Truck freight volume rebounds 3%+ month-over-month from trailing 6-month minimum; enter long XLI on release day close
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days OR if truck freight declines 1%+ in next month's report, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED truck freight updates monthly; rebounding cycles occur every 2-3 months during normal business cycles; 14-day exit window ensures capture of mean-reversion momentum.

data.source: FRED series MMNRNJ (Money Market Mutual Fund Shares weekly) and price_only XLF daily volatility via fred_series and price_only adapters

behavior: When money market fund inflows spike (new deposits into MMNRNJ) by 15%+ week-over-week during periods of elevated VIX, it signals panic flight-to-safety. This excess liquidity typically reverts as fear normalizes, creating 5-10 day bullish fade trades in financial equities.

market_impact.rationale: Money market inflows during stress compress funding spreads and reduce deposit competition for banks; stabilization allows equities to re-rate upward.

trade_logic.entry: MMNRNJ weekly inflow >15% above 4-week avg; VIX >25; XLF 20-day volatility >25%; enter long XLF
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if VIX drops 30%+ from entry level, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: FRED money market data updates weekly; stress events occur monthly; VIX spikes provide clear entry windows; panic reversions are typically 7-14 day events.

idea: Airline Load Factor Capacity Utilization Pop

data.source: BTS Airline Load Factor (monthly via bts_airline_load_factor adapter) and XLY price_only daily

behavior: When reported airline load factors exceed 85% (monthly BTS release), it signals demand strength. Consumer discretionary and hospitality stocks rally 3-7 days after release as investor sentiment shifts toward travel and leisure demand confidence.

market_impact.rationale: High airline load factors indicate strong leisure/business travel demand, benefiting consumer discretionary spending and hospitality valuations.

trade_logic.entry: BTS monthly load factor >85%; enter long XLY one trading day after release
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days OR if next month's load factor prints <82%, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: BTS releases load factor data monthly; capacity is consistently 83-87% in normal markets; seasonality creates predictable cycles; 7-day window captures post-release momentum.

data.source: SEC insider trading filings (Form 4 aggregate buys per sector, weekly via html_table adapter scrape of SEC EDGAR filings)

behavior: When insider buy volume (Form 4 filings) in a single sector exceeds prior 3-month average by 40%+ within a rolling 5-day window, it signals conviction-level buying by corporate officers. Equities typically outperform sector benchmarks by 2-5% in following 2-3 weeks as momentum builds.

market_impact.rationale: Concentrated insider buying in technology reflects executive conviction on valuation; historically precedes 15-30 day outperformance rallies.

trade_logic.entry: Aggregate Form 4 buy volume in XLK exceeds 3-month rolling avg by 40%+; enter long XLK at next day open
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days OR if insider buy volume returns to baseline, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: SEC filings update daily and aggregate weekly; insider conviction clusters occur every 2-3 weeks across sectors; 14-day windows align with insider position accumulation cycles.

data.source: XLU price_only daily (volatility, drawdown, momentum via price_only adapter)

behavior: When XLU 20-day realized volatility drops below 10% (multi-year low for defensive sector), and the ETF declines 3%+ from recent 20-day high, mean-reversion bias favors 5-10 day bullish rebounds as fear premiums collapse and dividend yields reactivate portfolio flows.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities compress during market risk-off; low volatility + small declines create mechanical dividend-capture and rotation-back-to-safety setups.

trade_logic.entry: XLU 20-day volatility <10%; XLU down 3%+ from 20-day high; enter long XLU
trade_logic.exit: After 8 trading days OR if XLU 20-day volatility rises above 15%, whichever first

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Volatility compression in XLU occurs monthly; 3% declines are routine in market turbulence; 8-day windows capture reversion peaks; setup fires 1-2x per month.
